note: task-start OWNS Phase 1 of the taskmaster pipeline: a structured dialogue that produces an immutable, testable spec (context capture, solution variants, file-change allowlist, acceptance criteria bound to oracles, red-team critic, tool-backed Definition-of-Ready gate) BEFORE any code is written. Near-misses are the downstream pipeline siblings (task-implement executes a ready spec, task-verify runs gates, task-deliver commits+PRs, task-fix-pr applies review feedback) plus debug (root-cause with no spec), migration-plan (a different planning artifact), adr (records a decision, not a work spec), and the lightweight track-* tracker (board cards, not a heavyweight spec). mock_rate encodes the IDEAL outcome for `--decider mock`; real deciders ignore it.

queries.text: start a task to add a rate limiter to the /api/login endpoint
let's spec this out properly before anyone writes code — we want to add webhook retries with exponential backoff

PROJ-482 needs to become a proper spec — go through the dialogue, pin down the file allowlist and testable criteria
I want to build a CSV export for the reports page but first let's do the whole context-capture + solution-variants thing and lock in a contract

before we dispatch this to the taskmaster executor I need an immutable spec — the feature is per-user feature flags via LaunchDarkly
help me scope out and formalize the work for splitting the monolith auth module into its own service — I need the DoR gate to pass before I start
kick off a new task from issue #217 — explore 3 approaches, bind each acceptance criterion to a test, then run the readiness gate

run the quality gates on this repo — tests, lint, type-check, build
verification passed, now commit everything and open the PR
the login page throws a 500 intermittently under load — figure out why

queries.note: understanding existing code → explain, not starting a task

queries.text: I have a rough idea for a notifications feature but no spec yet — start the task, we need to explore approaches and produce the contract before implementing
queries.note: mentions implementing but no spec exists yet → task-start authors it first, beats task-implement

queries.text: define this piece of work as a proper testable spec instead of just jumping in and coding it
queries.note: must beat migration-plan — this is a task spec with acceptance criteria, not a migration plan

queries.text: the spec is already written and green on the DoR gate — just execute it in this session

queries.text: we already agreed on the approach in the ADR — I just need the tests, lint and build to go green before I ship
queries.note: no spec authoring wanted; running the gates is task-verify
